management_tone.change_vs_prior: No período atual o tom evolui de 'resiliente e reflexivo' para 'confiante e orientado por execução': mantém a ênfase em disciplina e estratégia (3 pilares), mas acrescenta otimismo sobre começo de ano (momentum, melhora de margem e geração de caixa) em comparação ao trimestre anterior que focava mais em recuperação pós-pressões e construção de músculo.
justification_quotes: In tough moments, great companies and cultures find a way to get stronger, and that is what this quarter begins to show.
We started the year with momentum.
We made meaningful progress on the mission we set from day 1, even in a dynamic context that stress tested our strategy.

guidance_changes.summary: Guidance operacional central mantida: manutenção da orientação de Brazil Beer cash COGS per hectoliter (ex-marketplace) em aumento de 4.5% a 7.5% para 2026; capital allocation segue com recompra em curso e pagamento de IOC adicional. Não houve afrouxamento nem aperto explícito do guidance de custos comparado ao período anterior.
changes_vs_prior: Guidance de Brazil Beer cash COGS per hectoliter (ex-marketplace) mantida em 4.5%–7.5% (sem alteração vs. comunicação prévia).

Distribuição de caixa/retornos ao acionista segue ativa: execução do programa de recompra em curso e novo IOC declarado de BRL 700 million, adicionando-se ao pagamento de BRL 1.2 billion (tranche) — continuidade versus o intenso retorno a acionistas descrito no período anterior.
themes_vs_prior: Mesmo foco nos 3 pilares estratégicos (categoria, digital/ecossistema, disciplina operacional) — tema consistente entre trimestres.
Maior ênfase no início do ano: momentum comercial (premium, balanced, non-alcohol) e confiança no efeito calendar (Carnival, FIFA World Cup) enquanto mantém disciplina de custo e gestão de receita.

top_analyst_questions.question: My question is about net revenue per hectoliter in Brazil Beer. For us, at least it was the main highlight of the quarter, especially given how your volumes outperformed. So if you could qualify the 8% year-on-year increase, what drove that in terms of the contribution from mix? Any contribution from price increases in case they took place in the first quarter would also be very helpful. And also seasonally, Q1 usually has a weaker pricing than Q4, right, because of state taxes in Brazil. if there was anything different in this ICMS dynamic this year that may also, in part, explain the pricing performance in Brazil Beer. That would be my question.
top_analyst_questions.response_summary: A companhia atribuiu o aumento do net revenue per hectoliter a três componentes em hierarquia: carryover do programa de revenue management de 2025, ações de revenue management já em 2026, e contribuição de mix (crescimento consistente do segmento above core e premium). Sobre impostos (ICMS) disseram que 'não houve nada importante a destacar' no trimestre.
top_analyst_questions.response_quality: clara na narrativa dos drivers (carryover, RGM, mix) e com um posicionamento direto sobre ICMS; faltou uma decomposição numérica detalhada (percentuais exatos por componente) apesar de um percentual de mix e impacto aproximado terem sido comentados em outra resposta.
evidence_quotes: And first and foremost, in terms of hierarchy, the first component is exactly the -- all the effort put behind our revenue management agenda in '25 and the carryover we brought into the first quarter of '26.

The second component in the hierarchy comes from our revenue management agenda into '26, right? And the third component comes from the mix since our above core segment continued to grow in a very solid pace, right?
On the tax side, there was nothing that was important to highlight during the quarter. So same as last year.

top_analyst_questions.question: My question is really now jumping into volumes in Brazil Beer. In the presentation, we shared that the industry was down by mid-single digits in Q1. And at the same time, I think, Lisboa, you continue to suggest that you think this was due to cyclical factors such as the weather, right? And last quarter, marked, I think the fourth quarter in a row of falling industry volumes at a relatively steep pace. So in theory, starting now in Q2, we are cycling through that, I think, easy comparison base. And so my question to you, Lisboa, and I think you mentioned in different occasions in recent past, how confident you are about the potential of the category. And obviously, this year, you have a positive calendar effects such as the World Cup. So my question too is really how confident you are that we are cycling through this easy comparison base, how confident you are that volumes can grow in the balance of 2026 in the next 3 quarters?
top_analyst_questions.response_summary: Lisboa corrigiu que a empresa está comparando com a maior dificuldade (toughest comparison base) e reiterou que a fraqueza é explicada principalmente por fatores cíclicos (tempo/clima afetando ocasiões) mais do que por mudança estrutural na demanda. Indicou confiança numa recuperação gradual suportada por calendário (FIFA World Cup, feriados) e pela melhoria sequencial do portfólio e execução comercial.
top_analyst_questions.response_quality: convincente em explicar a natureza cíclica (clima/ocasiões) e em ligar recuperação a fatores calendáricos e execução comercial; porém não entregou uma previsão quantitativa de crescimento de volumes para os próximos trimestres (qualitativo e defensável, mas sem números de guidance de volume).
evidence_quotes: First, actually, the industry is cycling through the toughest comparison base, not the easiest comparison base, right?

the issue was not whether consumers wanted beer, but how often the right occasions happen, right, given the fact that what mostly impacted the industry were cyclical drivers, which is pretty much the same that happened in this quarter in '26, right, being the most relevant impact coming from the weather.

we do expect that, first and foremost, the calendar will bring a pretty interesting right ground for the industry to continue recovering moving forward, right? A combination of long holidays, a combination of FIFA World Cup...

top_analyst_questions.question: I just wanted to follow up a little bit on the COGS outlook. You could help us understand and frame maybe the cadence throughout the year. Obviously, we saw the pressure as in first half as an expected. But just given the current environment with higher aluminum prices, et cetera. How should we think about the cost flow through for you guys based on the hedges for the rest of the year?
top_analyst_questions.response_summary: Fleury reiterou a guidance para 2026 de Brazil Beer cash COGS per hectoliter (ex-marketplace) entre 4.5% e 7.5% e explicou que Q1 tende a ser o mais pressionado e que a evolução deve começar a aliviar a partir do segundo trimestre; disse que há hedges e projetos de eficiência em curso e que continuarão a gerenciar custos ativamente, sem alterar a orientação atual.
top_analyst_questions.response_quality: direta quanto à manutenção da faixa de guidance e à expectativa de alívio a partir do Q2; porém sem detalhar a sensibilidade ao preço do alumínio ou a profundidade dos hedges (comentário mais operacional/gestão que técnico).
evidence_quotes: When we're starting '26, what we have said to the market is our range between 4.5% for Brazil Beer cash COGS per hectoliter, excluding marketplace from 4.5% to 7.5%.
We know, and that's something that I also comment that we know that Q1 is probably the highest on cost, and that should start easing through the second quarter onwards.
we continue advancing on cost initiatives towards capturing efficiencies.

red_flags.quote: I cannot -- this is not a guidance. I cannot tell you how that will come over the year.
red_flags.rationale: Resposta do CFO evita fornecer clareza sobre o timing e formato futuro dos pagamentos/dividendos ao longo do ano — declaração explícita de não comentar sobre guidance nesta área.

red_flags.quote: I will keep the protocol in place. I'm going to answer 1 question to give you all the chance to interact with us, okay?
red_flags.rationale: Limitação do Q&A (responder apenas uma pergunta) altera o andamento da discussão e reduz a oportunidade de aprofundamento em temas levantados — mudança de escopo das perguntas originalmente feitas.

red_flags.quote: Can you hear me well? Just checking the mic here on my end. Great.
red_flags.rationale: Momento de hesitação/pausa técnica do executivo antes de responder que introduz atraso e quebra a fluidez da resposta (potencial perda de foco em seguida).

items: Net revenue per hectoliter growth around 8.3% in the quarter (strong price/mix performance).
Double-digit normalized EBITDA growth and 60 bps consolidated margin expansion despite FX and commodity headwinds.
Very strong operational cash flow: 'the strongest first quarter performance in the past 10 years' allowing continued buybacks/IOC execution.
surprise_score.justification: Os resultados operacionais (net revenue/hl ~8.3%, EBITDA crescimento de dois dígitos e expansão de margem) e a geração de caixa muito elevada no Q1 representam surpresas táticas moderadas porque mostram execução e resiliência em face de custos/FX adversos; são materialmente positivos para o curto prazo mas não alteram o guidance principal (COGS guidance foi mantida) nem mudam radicalmente a tese de investimento — por isso uma pontuação intermediária (6).
